title: TaskCompleted payload
description: Emitted when a task closes via task_complete. Captures the closing commit_sha (the structural link between the task and the work that resolved it) and an optional closure_summary. The envelope's rationale carries the agent's 'why this counts as done' — closure_summary is operational color (what landed, what's left for follow-on).

chain_slug.description: Chain slug disambiguating which chain's task this event targets, so the fold scopes to one task rather than fanning out across same-slug tasks in the project (bug task-lifecycle-event-folds-fan-out-across-duplicate-task-slugs). Optional: pre-fix events omit it and the fold falls back to the legacy slug+project match.

commit_sha.description: The commit SHA that landed the task's work, or the literal sentinel 'unversioned' for tasks whose artifacts live outside any git repo (user-level scripts, vault docs) per mcp__toolkit-server__work convention. Optional in this schema because the current task_complete handler accepts close-without-SHA (a friction-filing-reminder hook subsequently catches it at session-end audit, not at write time); future T3 tightening may gate on envelope.rationale or on a typed close-policy.

closure_summary.description: Free-text wrap-up: what landed, what's left, follow-on pointers. Distinct from envelope.rationale (which is the 'why this counts as done' enforced field); closure_summary is the human-readable operational note. Optional.
